popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description:
He was one of the few barons to remain loyal to Edward II, however, his corruption forced him being exiled along with his son, Hugh, the younger in 1321.

Edward II recalled them to England a year la …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester於1261年3月1日出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾時，他的父親Hugh Le Despenser37歲，母親Aline Basset33歲。他於大約1285年與Lady Isabella de Beauchamp在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1326年10月27日在英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托享年65歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester于1261年3月1日出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾时，他的父亲Hugh Le Despenser37岁，母亲Aline Basset33岁。他于大约1285年与Lady Isabella de Beauchamp在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1326年10月27日在英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托享年65岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester was born on 1 March 1261, in Gloucestershire, England, his father, Hugh Le Despenser, was 37 and his mother, Aline Basset, was 33. He married Lady Isabella de Beauchamp about 1285, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 27 October 1326, in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester est né le 1 mars 1261, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Le Despenser, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Aline Basset, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Lady Isabella de Beauchamp environ 1285, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 27 octobre 1326, à Bristol, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester am 1. März 1261 in Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh Le Despenser 37 und seine Mutter Aline Basset 33. Er hat um 1285 in England Lady Isabella de Beauchamp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 27. Oktober 1326 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England und wurde in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester è nato il il 1 marzo 1261 nel Gloucestershire, England, il suo padre, Hugh Le Despenser, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Aline Basset, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Lady Isabella de Beauchamp circa 1285, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 27 ottobre 1326, nel Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchesterが1261年3月1日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Hugh Le Despenserは37歳，母Aline Bassetは33歳であった。約1285年にイギリスでLady Isabella de Beauchampと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1326年10月27日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリストルで65歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester(이)가 1261년 3월 1일 영국 그루스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh Le Despenser(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Aline Basset(은)는 33세였다. 그는 대략 1285년 영국에서 Lady Isabella de Beauchamp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1326년 10월 27일 영국 그루스스터실르 브리스톨에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이에 묻혔다.

mn.1 March 1261–27 October 1326: 1261 оны Гуравдугаар сарын 1–1326 оны Аравдугаар сарын 27

pt.1 March 1261–27 October 1326: 1 de março de 1261–27 de outubro de 1326
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester nasceu em 1 de março de 1261, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Le Despenser, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Aline Basset, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Isabella de Beauchamp aproximadamente 1285, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de outubro de 1326, em Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester родился 1 марта 1261 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Hugh Le Despenser было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Aline Basset, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Isabella de Beauchamp около 1285 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 27 октября 1326 г. в местности бристол, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.1 March 1261–27 October 1326: 1 de marzo de 1261–27 de octubre de 1326
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester nació el 1 de marzo de 1261, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh Le Despenser, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Aline Basset, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Lady Isabella de Beauchamp aproximadamente 1285, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 27 de octubre de 1326, en Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
